body:
If your issue is **track correction** (door off-track, bent tracks, misaligned rollers, or a door that's crooked), I'd lean toward companies with a long track record of repair work rather than installers that primarily sell new doors.

Here are the East Bay companies that consistently stand out:

  1. **R &S Overhead Garage Door, Inc.** (San Leandro)

 * One of the longest-established garage door companies in Northern California.

 * Frequently recommended for repair work rather than replacement.

 * Good reputation for handling difficult mechanical issues, including track alignment and cable problems.

 * Community recommendations on Reddit also mention positive repair experiences. [Garage door stuck crooked (Inner Sunset) — recs for repair?](https://www.reddit.com/r/bayarea/comments/1mgeuyn/garage_door_stuck_crooked_inner_sunset_recs_for/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

  2. **All Bay Garage Doors**

 * Strong local reputation with a high volume of positive customer reviews.

 * Focuses on repairs, maintenance, and installations.

 * Often praised for honest diagnostics instead of immediately recommending a new door.

  3. **Real Garage Doors**

 * Excellent customer ratings and a strong reputation in the Fremont/Newark area.

 * Frequently mentioned for careful troubleshooting and quality repair work.

  4. **Garage Door Pros & Automatic Gates**

 * Highly rated in Contra Costa County.

 * Good reputation for spring, cable, and track repairs.

 * Responsive if you need same-day service.

  5. **Gil's Garage Door & Gate Services**

 * Very highly rated around Berkeley and Oakland.

 * Known for repair-first service and emergency calls.

### A note about Precision

**Precision Garage Door of Bay Area** has thousands of positive reviews overall and many satisfied customers. [](https://precisiondooreastbay.com/reviews?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[15 Best Oakland, CA Garage Door Repair Companies | Expertise.com](https://www.expertise.com/home-improvement/garage-doors/california/oakland?utm_source=chatgpt.com) However, some Bay Area homeowners on Reddit have reported feeling they were pushed toward more extensive repairs or replacements than expected. That's not definitive, but if you call them, I'd recommend getting a second estimate before approving major work. [Garage door repair](https://www.reddit.com/r/eastbay/comments/1orgp3x/garage_door_repair/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

For **track correction specifically** , I'd rank them:

  1. R&S Overhead Garage Door, Inc.

  2. All Bay Garage Doors

  3. Real Garage Doors

One important note: if your garage door has come **off the track** , don't keep trying to run the opener. A door that's off-track often has a cable or roller under uneven load, and operating it can make the damage worse or create a safety hazard. [4 Signs Your Garage Door Is Right About to Break (Even If It Seems Fine), According to Repair Experts](https://www.thespruce.com/signs-your-garage-door-is-about-to-break-11985908?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

If you tell me **which East Bay city you're in** (Oakland, Berkeley, Walnut Creek, Concord, Fremont, etc.) and **what happened** (door off-track, bent vertical track, roller popped out, hit by a car, etc.), I can narrow it down to the best specialist for that specific repair.  

Give feedback
